content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

April 17, 2002

Lawrence A. Davis, I
Booking No. 7068556
BKS No. 122 Dorm 7100
P.O. Box 86164 Terminal Annex
Los Angeles, CA 90012

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-092

Dear Mr. Davis:

Thank you for your general inquiry regarding the criteria needed for enacting emergency measures within the State of California. As specified on our web site, the role of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”) is as follows:

The mission of the FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION is to promote the integrity of representative state and local government in California through fair, impartial interpretation and enforcement of political campaign, lobbying, and conflict of interest laws.

Further, regulation 18329(b)(8)(D) provides that the Commission may decline to provide advice where the request does not present a question under the Political Reform Act. (See also, Reg. 18329(c)(4)(F), copy enclosed.) Thus, we would not be the agency to respond to your request.

I suggest you contact the Office of Governor for specifications on enactment of executive orders and/or emergency regulations. The Office of the Secretary of State would be the contact for questions relating to initiative certification (www.ss.ca.gov).

I hope this information will assist you in your search.

Very truly yours,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

Joan Giannetta
Legal Secretary

1 Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109 – 18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
